title: CONTRIBUTING.md and open source license
description: Add a CONTRIBUTING guide covering local dev setup, PR expectations, and code style, plus finalize and add an open source LICENSE file to the repository.
acceptanceCriteria: `LICENSE` file exists at repo root with a standard OSI-approved license text (recommend MIT for maximum adoption ease, consistent with the project's zero-cost/open ethos — confirm final choice with the project owner)

`CONTRIBUTING.md` documents: local dev setup (pointing to TASK-7's dev compose file), running lint/typecheck/tests (TASK-8), branch/PR conventions, and how to propose changes to `packages/schema`'s shared types safely (since they're a cross-cutting contract)

steps.details: Confirm with the project owner whether MIT (recommended default for this kind of infra tool) or another OSI license is intended; add the corresponding `LICENSE` file text with the correct copyright holder name and year.
Update the root and per-workspace `package.json` `license` fields to match the chosen license identifier (e.g. `"MIT"`).
In `CONTRIBUTING.md`, document cloning the repo, `pnpm install`, `docker-compose up` (TASK-7) for local dev, and running `pnpm -r run lint/typecheck/test` (TASK-8) before opening a PR.

steps.description: Document PR conventions and schema-change caution
steps.details: Add a section noting that changes to `packages/schema` types affect server, admin, and the publishable `connector`/`preview-kit` packages, and should be called out explicitly in PR descriptions.
Ensure TASK-62's README links to both `LICENSE` and `CONTRIBUTING.md`.

technicalNotes: License choice has real legal/adoption implications — treat step 1's confirmation as a genuine decision point with the project owner, not a default to silently apply.
